reproducibility: Each entry pins a real repo + vulnerable/patched commit SHAs; the detector runs over the real target file(s) at those SHAs. Pin-or-drop: an unresolvable entry is dropped from the denominator with a reason, never counted missed, never hand-faked. sink_probe values are refined against the real vuln file at detector-build time.

result.measured_on: real target files at pinned vuln/patched SHAs (line-precise config rule: an unhardened XML parser flagged in vuln, not in patched)
result.miss_note: Both misses are ATYPICAL fixes where the static XXE signal persists in the patched tree — dom4j adds a safe ALTERNATIVE (createDefault() in SAXReader.java) while leaving the flagged SAXHelper.createXMLReader path, and JDOM2 REORDERS existing setFeature calls (present in both versions). A pure config rule (and mainstream SAST) flags both versions; discriminating them needs dataflow/ordering analysis. Documented improvement-loop fixtures. The standard in-place-hardening shape (Tika seed, python-docx) is rediscovered.

note: XXE spans Java (DocumentBuilderFactory/SAXParserFactory/XMLInputFactory/SAXReader) and Python (lxml etree.XMLParser). The detector is multi-language: tree-sitter-java for Java, ast for Python. Calibrated on the Java Tika seed.

seed.sink: StAX XMLInputFactory in getXMLInputFactory() used on untrusted XML with DTDs + external general entities enabled
seed.authoritative_source: NVD CVE-2025-66516 (CWE-611, CVSS 9.8); same vuln as CVE-2025-54988

heldout.sink: SAXReader/SAXParserFactory.newInstance() default config parses untrusted XML with external entities/DTD enabled
SAXBuilder.configureParser builds an XMLReader over untrusted XML with DTD/external entities
heldout.patched_shape: REORDER: apply the disallow-doctype-decl/external-entity features LAST so they take effect (features were present but overridden) — likely a HARD discrimination case (both versions contain the setFeature calls)

heldout.sink: etree.XMLParser(remove_blank_text=True) — lxml parser with resolve_entities defaulting True, parsing untrusted .docx XML
heldout.patched_shape: add resolve_entities=False to the etree.XMLParser construction

denominator_note: Generalization denominator = 3 pinned held-out CVEs (dom4j, JDOM2, python-docx). JDOM2's fix is a REORDER (features present in both versions) so it is expected hard/miss — the honest generalization measure. A 5th candidate (from the research workflow) can be added if it resolves cleanly.
